candidates.stmt:
Lived in the borough of Sutton for more than 30 years . Three ( now adult) children educated in local state schools Retired former Trade Union National Officer Graduate of Strathclyde University (BA Hons) and the University of Kent (MA)
Particular interests in the provision of affordable housing and a positive economic policy to fund the public services we all depend on.

Living in Beddington for many years, I am passionate about this lovely and historic area of Sutton.

My children went to school here, play in the parks, and we shop, walk and use local services here. Actively involved in keeping the area healthy, green and clean as someone who cares, I have participated in  Neighbourhood Watch and the Beddington North Neighbourhood Forum. I have represented residents and spoken on our behalf at Council and other meetings, to stop the incinerator, save post offices and St Helier and stop development at Richmond Green.

I want to keep our community together, stop our local services being eroded, and make sure Beddington residents voices and views are heard.

As I am currently volunteering with a homeless charity, which also provides services for vulnerable adults, my interest, both politically, and personally lies with the provision of better mental health support for both the prevention of homelessness, and safety of vulnerable adults in the London Borough of Sutton. Equally, the need for better mental health provision generally across the country.

I grew up on a council estate in the midlands before moving to Sutton nearly 20 years ago. My dad drove buses and lorries, my grandad was a coal miner. I believe passionately in working hard for our community - something I already do as a senior front bench Councillor in Sutton.

I’ve earned a reputation locally as a diligent community activist, using my background in IT, global data networks, and project management to focus on the detail that really impacts the lives of local residents - without losing sight of the bigger picture.

I am a local resident who lives nearby in Sutton for over 12 years and who regular works in the Wimbledon, Raynes and Park and Morden areas, in fact my partners family have lived and worked in the area for decades.

I am a businessman with over a twenty of experience having founded and run an award winning inventory report business that serves tenants, landlords and lettings agents in London and the surrounding areas. I built an inventory report App that is available in over 58 languages and is used extensively by my own business and many others in the UK, which is award winning and a market leader and I have helped many dozens of people set up and run their own successful businesses.

I am not a career politician like my opponents but I am passionate about politics. I am in my early 50’s divorced with one Son and have been engaged to my partner of 12 years. I was born in Rochdale, Lancashire to a typical working class family, joined the Army at 19, served for 12 years and I am a Gulf War veteran. I am lucky to have seen many of life’s facets from a frugal upbringing, to serving my country to the benefits of running a successful business. Out of work I am a keen scuba diver and family man. I would be delighted to meet you personally to introduce ourselves properly.

I was born and educated in Sutton. I returned to Sutton in 2008 and live in Belmont ward.
Since 2008 I have been a volunteer at a local charity where I have helped
hundreds of residents with claims for Attendance Allowance, consumer issues and many other problems, sometimes saving them hundreds of pounds.
I also represent patients from my GP practice at the Patients’ Representative Group for the South West London area of the NHS.
My experience in these roles made me aware that many disabled people found it almost impossible to complete the on line application for a Blue Badge. Last year I managed to persuade council officials to provide face-to-face help with online applications where needed.
I have recently become a mentor supporting a troubled young person to
achieve her potential.
As your councillor I would be able to use the skills I have acquired to help
residents struggling with bureaucracy.
I am a life-long campaigner against all forms of racism and an active supporter of Sutton Friends of Palestine.

I live in Belmont with my family. Both my daughters were born in St Helier Hospital and educated at Cheam High where I was an elected parent-governor. With two teenage grandsons, I am aware that we need to provide more facilities for young people in the area. I welcome the Labour government’s funding of more neighbourhood officers to focus on antisocial behaviour such as misuse of e-bikes and weed smoking in public areas. I am also keen to see the Council provide more social housing to address the increasing problem of homelessness in our Borough.

Elaine Kwamin is a Sutton resident with a strong interest in governance, public service, and institutional accountability, with a focus on how systems and public bodies deliver fair, transparent, and practical outcomes for individuals and communities. She is also interested in international development and the UK’s role in global partnerships. Elaine believes in strengthening public services, supporting economic opportunity, and ensuring that policy works in practice for the people it is designed to serve.

Jake has lived in Carshalton for most of his life. He went to school at Carshalton Boys Sports College, and saw first hand the transformative effect Sutton's excellent teachers can have. His main interests are improving local transport, local history, improving mental health services and education.

I stood in Wallington North in the local elections 2018. I want to ensure local people are consulted and represented when it comes to making decisions about our local area, schools, hospitals, services and green spaces; that expert advice is sought and heeded and that changes are not being made outside of our control.

I have lived in Sutton and Carshalton for about 15 years having grown up in New Malden and then studying at Kingston University and the University of Humberside gaining a Fine Art degree. I still love being creative, but my interest in the environment and sustainability lead me to pursue a career in this field.

I have worked for local charities EcoLocal and Bioregional and have also volunteered at Sutton Ecology Centre. I worked for a number of years at the Energy Saving Trust based in London and now work for the Wandle Trust as their Education officer, leading outdoor sessions with children from local schools, engaging them with their local river and environment and learning outside of the classroom. I love my job!

I am very proud of our borough’s local green spaces, the beautiful river Wandle running through them and the many community organisations that help to enrich our local area.My partner and I love living in Carshalton and my interest, passion and concern for our environment has grown even more since having our two children and looking to their future, and beyond. They currently attend a local school in Carshalton.

I am also a keen allotment gardener and enjoy growing our own fruit and vegetables.

Source: https://sutton.greenparty.org.uk/news/2019/03/03/greens-select-verity-thomson-as-candidate/

I have lived in the borough all my life, going to school here. A retired scientist, working in International Development, I am passionate about Fair Trade and promoting co-operative values. I was a family carer for 9 years, active in the Community Health Council and influenced borough carers' policies. I am a former Board member of Co-operatives UK and the Co-op Group.

I have lived in Carshalton with my family for over 30 years, my grandchildren attend local schools. I have volunteered for charities working with families in need and animal rescue, and am a trustee for a charity supporting school and community education.
As a dog owner I'm keen to protect our local green spaces. We desperately need more affordable housing and a much bigger proportion of this should be social housing. We need to balance housing needs with protecting green belt land and our natural environment.

candidates.p: The Christian Party Christian Peoples Alliance
candidates.stmt:
'Whistleblower fined £ x 000s by CPS for exposing same-sex gender abortion'

'HM's Govt signs into law same-sex marriage'

‘Since 1967, nearly nine million foeti lost to the nation through legalised abortion’

The NUT has called for children as young as two to be taught about LGBT issues.  See also www.thetimes.co.uk/article/call-to-teach-toddlers-about-transgender-issues-h2kkdcjlq The UK still a Christian Country?

Vote for change.  Vote Christian Peoples Alliance

I have been a member of the Labour Party for over 45 years and prior to my retirement 7 years ago was a secondary school  teacher for over 40 years in east and northwest London and was also a member of the NUT and latterly the NEU which I still belong to as a retired member. Apart from education I maintain an interest in transport matters and volunteer regularly at the London Transport Museum in Covent Garden. I pursue my interests through memberships of several heritage railways, the National Trust, English Heritage, the RSPB, CAMRA, Surrey Wildlife and the WWT.

I'm a retired professor of atmospheric physics and have lived in the borough for 20 years. For several years I was chair of the Governors of a Lambeth Comprehensive School. I have a strong interest in the environmental and work with the Woodland Trust.

I’ve lived in Cheam for most of my life, went to school here and have mainly worked in radio, in fact I was one of the original team that started our local station Radio Jackie, sadly leaving during Covid.  I still live here and understand the practical issues that can affect us, for example on bin collection days having bins thrown back in gardens, with food waste strewn on the ground and the subsidence issues affecting many places like Churchill Road and surrounding homes.  It’s a privilege to stand, and I hope you can support me in the voting booth.

I'm John Offen I've lived in the Borough of Sutton for 20 years. I was born in NZ and moved to London in the 70s where I met my wife and raised our family. 
We support Labour as the best way for working people to have a voice in a world noisy with non-dom billionaires and media moguls. It's crucial we have representation that will look after our NHS and resist those keen to sell it to private and overseas interests. 
Sutton as a Borough is such a nice place with great people, I wouldn't live anywhere else. 
I have represented my neighbours before, urging the council to pay a fair price to local homeowners forced to move by compulsory purchase. 
I believe that injustice and unfairness should always be called out and challenged.

Tom works for a housing association and is a union representative. He is deeply involved in the local community, sitting on the Mitcham Common conservator's and a local residents association.
He grew up near Wrythe green, was born in St.helier,  and now lives in New Mill Quarter with his wife and two cats, Cherry and Mango. He attended a local school where he received speech and language support. He is standing for council because he wants to give back to the community that "has given him so many opportunities".

Dave was elected in 2022 by a huge majority of people who trusted him to deliver the results and he has. Having lived in Hackbridge with his family for 20 years.  He is well known locally for his involvement in projects to improve the area, and for holding the Council to account.

Dave’s won investment in excess of £220,000 for a new play park, infrastructure and security. As well as leading on flower planting, street trees, fetes and various community projects.

He’s led the fight to tackle moped crime, road safety,  flooding, pot holes and is a leading voice against the Incinerator.

His professional career in Public Health means he’s confident to speak up for his neighbours.

“Hackbridge is my community, like you I want it respected, invested in and protected. Having spent the last four years as your Councillor successfully improving the area, I want to complete what I started".

Brendan is married with grown up children, who all went to local schools. He works as an NHS GP and has previously been Mayor of Sutton and a Councillor. He is passionate about improving health and social care services, especially for children and older people.

Margaret Thomas is a dedicated community advocate with years of experience "at the heart of the community," she is well-known for managing the Middleton Circle library and founding essential local initiatives including job clubs, an over-60s lunch club, and a new mothers' group. Her candidacy is driven by a deep passion for reducing poverty and inequality, having spent decades supporting Sutton's most vulnerable residents. A proven "community candidate" who prioritises local needs over political showmanship, Margaret is committed to improving conditions for all Sutton residents and ensuring their voices are heard within the council. Her family live in Hackbridge and her grandchildren are at local schools.

Alan Plant has lived in Sutton for 40 Years.

A local Residents Association leader, instrumental in combating dilapidated parts of the Borough and defending residents against over-developments by construction companies. Responsible for the introduction of a 20MPH zone in a residential area.

A Neighbourhood Watch Ward Coordinator, working with the local Police Safer Neighbourhood Team and responsible for implementing a "No Cold Call Zone"

A Volunteer with Sutton`s Citizens Commissioners, ensuring the resident`s voice is heard

Make your vote count on the 3rd May, by electing those that really care about Sutton.

I have lived in the Borough since I was 9 months old, went to Nonsuch & was deeply involved in Cheam’s historic house, Whitehall.

I am a lecturer in adult education & a qualified London Blue Badge Tourist Guide.

I am very concerned about the effect of the cost-of-living crisis on Sutton’s residents & believe Labour representation on Sutton Council is vital to improve local services & provide the best service for all the borough’s residents

Ed lives on Beddington Lane and has been a Councillor in Beddington South since 2010. He is a qualified teacher who has taught Computer Science and Economics at two local state secondary schools. He also currently serves as a governor of SCOLA.

It's time to vote Conservative for a sharper focus on delivering the very best public services in the most efficient way. Wasteful and careless spending takes money away from spending on important areas that could benefit the community. Fostering economic growth is the other key area that completes the picture and that can be as simple as getting decent traffic management, so that we don't all waste hours in jams!

Mark has lived in the borough for 30 years. He is a Database Manager at a charity that promotes the arts in prisons and other secure settings and previously worked for the Sutton Library Service. He is an ex-actor with a ‘Pointless’ trophy and a love of cinema and history.

I have lived in Sutton for over 25 years, raising my family here and putting down deep roots in the community I am proud to call home. Throughout that time I have seen first hand what makes Sutton a friendly, welcoming borough, and I am committed to protecting and strengthening those values.
Guided by a strong work ethic and Labour Party values of integrity, fairness and accountability, I am standing to serve my local community and to ensure the council delivers on its responsibilities.
I will work constructively, but firmly, to hold the council to account and to help Sutton continue to thrive for current residents and future generations.

Dean was born at St Helier Hospital, has a lifelong association with Sutton and now lives in Chiltern Road. His work in the charity and faith sector has given him a wide knowledge of issues such as social care and adult and child protection. Dean and his wife are proud to be raising their three children in the borough and he is keen to see his local community flourish.

I’m a Certified Public Accountant with my office based at Rosehill roundabout. I am supporting local residents and small businesses across st Helier west for many years. I know the cost-of-living pressures families and shopkeepers face, because I work with them every day. I’m standing for the Green Party because I believe St Helier West deserves a local counselor who listens and acts. I have raised safety and vandalism issues so many times for businesses around my area and still in touch with council to solve ASB issues and I have already campaigned to protect St Helier Hospital services from further cuts, and I raised a petition for a zebra crossing on Thornton road to keep our children, parents and elderly people stay safe on busy local roads.
As an accountant, I’ll bring financial common sense to Sutton Council - making sure your money is spent wisely on the things that matters most to local residents : a hospital we can rely on, warm affordable homes, safer streets, and real support for local businesses where they thrive and survive without feeling any kind of violence and risk.

I have created a very strong bond with local residents and business around Saint Helier over the years, being their local resident and local accountant.
My concern and love for these locals led me further to take this initiative to be their representative in council to strengthen this connection stronger and stronger as a family member.  I am a practical person who will ask council to solve these issues on spot rather than treating residents as employees.

For last 1-2 decades I believe residents have been ignored, there is no real human emotional connection between council and them, crumbling Saint Helier Hospital is being used as a political tool. Council is being run as a Company rather than a local council. 
I want to create a live human vibrant connection between council and residents that proves their voice is heard, listened to and practical steps are being taken to improve their

I want to give something back to the place that has given me so much. I grew up in this borough and went to school here and my family has long standing ties to St Helier West. My dad ran a shop in the ward years ago so this community has always been part of my life. I have worked in the NHS and for the London Borough of Sutton in procurement and I now work within London's public transport sector. My career has always been in public service and I have seen first hand how important it is for services to be efficient and focused on the people who rely on them. St Helier Hospital means a great deal to me and to local people and I will always stand up for the services our community depends on. I care about people and I listen and I am not afraid to fight for what is right. I want to be a steady and reliable voice for residents.

As an experienced a housing worker I am well placed to take up residents’ housing concerns. 
As a former Deputy Leader, Chief Whip and Finance Chair of a neighbouring Council, I have the experience to be your voice on the Council from day one. I will speak up for tenants and residents at Council and Committee meetings, and remain accountable to the people of St. Helier West.
If elected I will work closely with the Police, business leaders, health authority, local schools and community organisations to make St. Helier West a better place to live, work and play.

I was born in Carshalton and have lived in the borough for most of my life. I worked in construction for several years before moving into rail maintenance as a track worker. I am a committed trade unionist and proud to represent other track workers in helping to keep them safe in the work place and protect their pay and conditions. Having worked on zero hour and agency contracts in the past I believe in permanent, secure jobs for all and that above all hard work should pay well - too many public services are outsourced to contracting firms whose focus is on enriching their shareholders rather than providing good services for local residents.
I am fiercely proud of St Helier Hospital, where my dad passed away last year, and of the people who work there. It is a central part of the community as well as an essential service and I would oppose any attempt to move any part of its provision to other parts of the borough.
I firmly believe in democracy, diversity and a fair slice of the cake for everyone, regardless of where they started in life and would welcome the chance to represent you as your ward councillor.

Retired Casting Director.  Voting member of BAFTA.  I have experience of being employed, self-employed and running a small, limited company.  Lived in Sutton for over 50 years.   Went to school in Cheam & college in Carshalton.  2 sons who both went to Sutton schools including Glenthorne where I was on the Parent Council.  I have personal experience struggling to get SEND support
 
A lot of experience juggling childcare & later care for disabled husband and elderly parents.   I’m passionate about supporting carers (Young & Adult) practically and emotionally.  I know just how much difference contact with The Sutton Carers’ Centre can make.  
 
Like most of us, I’m concerned about the vulnerability of children growing up in Care and  the transition as they leave the Care system.  I would be an advocate working to assist them to live positive, independent lives and be valued by our community
 
I’m a member of my GP’s Patient Participation Group and the Sutton-wide Patient Representation Group which holds regular meetings organised by Sutton Healthwatch.  I want our local patients’ voice to be heard nationally.  We must have a say in the NHS 10 Year Plan especially regarding waiting lists, access to GP appointments, speedy referrals, diagnoses and of course safe hospitals.
 
I’m a member of Sutton Community Fairtrade Group, Sutton Community Farm and have signed up to get involved in a Sutton Community Energy group.  The Co-op Party’s work with Labour has improved the potential for community ownership.  I want local businesses to thrive.  By working with Police and Trading Standards we need to root out criminal activity whether in illegal sales or shoplifting.

I have lived in LB Sutton for over 25 years. I am now retired, but worked as a civil servant, trade union official and parliamentary researcher - all of those roles gave me skills which I would be able to use in representing residents' interests, challenging poor Council decisions and  taking up issues on behalf of local people. 
I gained an MA degree in 2019 as a mature student. Last year gave me some insight into how the NHS works in our area - brilliantly as far as I was concerned.
I'm a longstanding member of the Labour and Co-operative parties, and believe we can make a huge difference by supporting new local businesses on our high streets. There are huge international challenges facing us now, but we must ensure we do the best for our communities.

Ed has lived in Sutton for twenty years and works at the Royal Marsden Hospital, having previously worked at St Helier. Edward has a special interest in keeping Sutton a low crime area and recognises the need for good quality housing, especially for young people and families.

I’m Chantelle and I’ve proudly called Sutton home for four years. It’s a fantastic town- but too many residents aren’t seeing the benefits of what it has to offer.  
Instead, we’ve seen declining safety, falling standards and a growing sense that local voices are being ignored. 
From youth services and healthcare to parking, culture and community safety - these aren’t small issues. They’re everyday concerns and they need urgent action.  
I'm committed to tackling crime, restricting the use of e-bikes on our High Street and making Sutton a clean, safe place to live.
If nothing changes, nothing improves. So if you’re ready to raise standards, be heard and see real action- not more of the same- vote Labour this May.

As a Sutton resident I'm keen to see local services improved. Sutton's roads are in a bad state and potholes are taking too long to repair. The Labour Government are doubling funding for road repairs so let's see some action in Sutton! I'll lobby for improved public transport services, to restore the S4 20 minute interval and to get more fast trains to Victoria. We need to improve recycling rates in Sutton - better communication from the Council on how to reduce contamination of recycling, along with more recycling bins for flats and better labelling.

I have lived in the centre of Sutton for the last 8 years. When I chose to move to Sutton from Merton, I came for the parks and green spaces; for the great local cafes, venues and shops; and the excellent transport links to central London where I work.

My love for Sutton is why I’m standing for council. It’s a good place to live and work, but it could be better. I’ll continue to be involved in local campaigns to end violence against women and girls, to safeguard our local services, keep our local hospital and to make our town centre safer for all, and I’m a strong believer in community action. Together we can work to create safer streets and cut crime.

We’ve had a Lib Dem-led Council for years - and these issues remain. It’s time for the voices of Sutton Central residents to be heard, it’s time for another Labour voice on the Council.

My name is Kehinde Akintunde. I’m the Assistant branch secretary for the GMB Union in Tower Hamlets; a role that I have enjoyed performing for the last 12 years as it has enabled me to develop skills in employment law and organisational responsibility.

I am standing as I believe in providing representation and support for the marginalised in our society. Therefore a vote for me not being a mouthpiece for the status quo but a vote for someone who believes passionately in making a difference in my community.

A vote for me ensures we support local businesses, our young people and develop programmes such as a work path for local residents ensuring that everyone feels a valued member of our community.

I have lived inSutton since 1978 when I moved from Wales to work in London.  I worked in the Civil Service – trade & industry – and before retiring six years ago I had spent a few years working in local government – adult social care with a focus on disability issues.  My children and grandson were born in Sutton.  Sutton has been a great place for me and my family to live and I want the same for other families. I believe that Labour will work to make this area even better.

Richard has lived locally for thirty years and his daughters went to local state schools. He retired after a successful career in public service and has been Councillor for Sutton South for eight years. He was Mayor of Sutton in 2016, raising £26,000 for charity. He still finds time to play the trumpet in a Sutton concert band.

Trish has been a Sutton South Councillor for four years. She worked in the insurance industry for 35 years and recently changed career into recruitment. She sat on the PTA at her youngest daughter's local State school for seven years, and has fundraised for several local charities.

Manish brings a strong professional background as an IT specialist with extensive experience delivering mission-critical projects across both public and private sectors. With an educational foundation that includes a degree in Engineering and an MBA, Manish is passionate about applying the same high standards to local government—ensuring services are efficient, modern, and designed around the needs of residents.
Beyond professional life, Manish has played an active role in the community for many years, as he organised resident participation in community cultural events including the High Street Christmas Light Switch-On, the Festival of Colours, and the Festival of Lights. These events have brought residents together, strengthened community bonds, and celebrated Sutton’s diversity.
Manish has lived in Sutton since 2005 and is proud to champion the Royal Marsden, Sutton United Football Club, and the borough’s exceptional schools, which contribute significantly to community life and local identity.

Priorities for Sutton and the Ward
- Transparency, Accountability, and Value for Money for Council Tax
- Revive 30 mph on main/trunk roads
- Introduce some free parking around high streets to boost small business
- Fix potholes
- Protect green spaces
- Boost cultural ecosystem in Sutton
- Boost AI education for youth in schools/colleges
- Consult on a vision for Sutton for next 5/10 years
- Review if Sutton needs more flat blocks

I've lived in Sutton for most of my life, having been born in St Helier, and I attended local primary and secondary schools. I've been involved in various local campaign groups for nearly twenty years with specific interests in human rights and the impact of inequality on people's lives.  I'm a member of Unison and work in Higher Education, so have seen at first hand the impact of fees on the aspirations of students who would like to attend university.  I want to ensure that our local services are not eroded further and that St Helier is saved on its current site.

Born and raised in Cardiff, then a History Degree from Oxford University, before a teaching career, a Head of 6th Form and a Deputy Head, before Head of Publications and Editor of 'Teaching Today' journal foe the Teachers; Union of which I had been elected National President. A Sutton resident for 30 years, I have served as a Magistrate and an 'Appropriate Adult' for Sutton Council looking after young people and 'vulnerable' adults who had been arrested.

I am Christopher Woolmer and I have lived in Sutton since 2002. I am a professional and performing musician and also teach at various schools in the London area, including in Sutton. I studied the organ and the piano at the Royal College of Music.

I am passionate about protecting the environment and human health against pollution. I have been involved in much local campaigning and am an ardent supporter of the importance and strength of local communities, as well as upholding the values of  Liberal Democracy, and I want to bring these values to Sutton Council.

My passion is education and opportunities for young people. I've served as chair of governors at a Sutton primary school, worked at a senior level for the National Education Union and now help charities like Centrepoint build better futures for young people. With my son at a Sutton sixth form, I know how important youth services, part-time work and safe streets are for teenagers so they can start their lives with confidence.

Colin was born in St Helier Hospital and has lived in The Wrythe for 37 years. He has been the Lead Councillor for Adult Social Services since he was elected in 2006 and in 2013 took on the Public Health role as well. Colin is the Managing Partner of the largest GP partnership in London, looking after 60,000 patients. He is an active member of Sutton Royal British Legion.

I am a father and an engineer. I've been working in the TV industry, radio telemetry, transport and for security services.
My natural political home is the labour party. The labour party's history represents fairness, decency and equality. Long may it provide good governance for this country.

I have lived in the borough for many years. A teacher by training, I was a Deputy Headteacher for many years before becoming a school improvement adviser. I am deeply passionate about education in our country and ensuring all of our young people get the best possible start in life.

I believe in democratic local accountability and will work tirelessly to ensure the Council work closely with all schools regardless of their status, academy or maintained.  I oppose all cuts to services from our local hospital and privatisation of the NHS.

Maria Qanita is a senior policy and operations professional with over a decade of experience across government, international advocacy and the non-profit sector. She has extensive experience working with communities, public institutions and decision-makers. As a local resident, she is committed to strengthening public services, supporting families and ensuring safer, cleaner and more inclusive neighbourhoods. Her priorities include improving local schools and youth opportunities, tackling the cost of living, addressing housing and street safety concerns, and ensuring residents’ voices are heard in council decisions.

Sunita has lived in Wallington with her family for almost 40 years and has spent her working life in media. She is a governor of Beddington Infant School and South Thames Colleges Group. As a Councillor, Sunita has helped secure investment to improve facilities around the ward and has lobbied on public health and community policing issues.

I was born, educated and married in the area and now live on Stanley Park Road with my family. 
I have been a local Scout leader in Wallington for 25 years and have worked in the travel industry, including running my own online travel business, for the last 30 years.
I will speak up against cuts to our local police by the Conservatives, demand action to make the junction at Wallington Green safer and work with residents to resolve parking issues

I’ve lived and worked in the London Borough of Sutton since 1995 as a business administrator, artist, advocate, and, more recently, a broadcaster. British-born with a multicultural heritage, I celebrate our cultural diversity.

Since 2008, I have worked as a Community Arts Practitioner, bringing people together through playful creativity, delivering participatory arts projects on local high streets and parks, reaching thousands of residents, and co-creating the Sutton Mosaic Memorial Bench in honour of female victims of male violence.

I define community as “communication in unity through diversity and inclusion.” I engage with people from all walks of life – children, teens and families, survivors of domestic abuse, rough sleepers, excluded pupils, neurodiverse learners, ESOL students, and older residents facing isolation. These experiences have reinforced my belief that every voice deserves to be heard and that kindness, connection, and empathy matter.

In that time, I also co-founded the Sutton Home Education Forum, chaired Arts Network Sutton, and sat on the board of Sutton Housing Partnership, and more.

For all of us, and our children, I have hope, and that is worth standing for. If elected, my efforts may have even more impact for the residents and businesses in Wallington North.

Thank you for considering me.

Tim was born and educated in Wallington and returned to live in the borough in 2001 with his wife and adult daughters. Recently retired from a broad career in technology and digital transformation, Tim was Chair of the Board of Governors at Beddington Infants School for a number of years.
I’ve lived in the borough of Sutton for almost 20 years, my son going through Stanley Park infants & Juniors and now in Oaks park.   My priorities are Education, Housing and helping with SEND issues.

Our family has lived in Carshalton and Wallington for 42 years. Our children attended Stanley Park Primary School and then Greenshaw High School, where I taught for over 20 years. I have served on several governing bodies. I have been an active member of the Labour Party for over 40 years. My expertise is predominantly in Education but I have St Helier and the NHS as well as housing and social needs high on my list of priorities.
As a Labour candidate I am standing on our locally agreed policies and pledges:

1. We will focus on the cost of living not see you go further into debt. We will ensure all residents are aware of the cost of living improvements the Government is making and actively encourage take up.Locally we will provide secure, quality job opportunities and attract new business - especially cooperatives and mutual - into our borough and promote wider business commitment to the London Living Wage. We will also seek full implementation of the Employment Act and support enforcement where we are legally able. Poverty and the need for food banks should never be allowed to happen in Sutton.

2. We will support increased locally based Policing to tackle crime and support victims. We will invest in CCTV, better lighting and community safety hubs and work with the community to improve women’s safety locally.

3. We will provide secure affordable homes for our younger people who are priced out of the borough and build more genuinely affordable council housing in our borough.

4. We will commit to: Cleaner Streets. Cleaner Air. "Community Spring Cleans" and monitored local "pop up skips". We will look at ways to withdraw from the Beddington Incinerator and its waste disposal contract that the Lib Dems shockingly brought to this borough.

5. We will always fully consult residents over parking and traffic schemes and seek strong local consent. Future schemes should be resident petition driven, not a simple statistical exercise by Council officers.

6. We will protect

I was born at St Helier hospital, grew up in Wallington, and have chosen to raise my family here.

I qualified as a Chartered Accountant in public sector audit, scrutinising how councils spend public money. Over the past 15 years I’ve worked across both public and private sectors, delivering real improvements in how organisations serve the people who depend on them.

I want to bring those same skills to Wallington North, holding spending decisions to account, cutting through waste, and making sure residents get the services they pay for.

Jayne, a chartered accountant, has lived in Wallington for 20 years, with her three boys all attending local schools. She has served as a local Councillor for the past 12 years, working to address local issues and improve the area. She is proud to have seen the community all working together to make the Wallington Music Festival and Festive Light celebrations popular and established events.

Sam works for a national charity which helps build communities and tackle loneliness and isolation. In previous roles he has raised over £2m for health and family causes. In his spare time he plays football and enjoys jogging around Sutton's parks. As a Councillor, Sam will work to improve transport links and support the voluntary sector.

Muhammad lives locally with his family. He has been very active in the local community for many years, most recently as a Wallington South Councillor. He served as Mayor of Sutton in 2015/2016 and is currently Vice Chair of the Planning Committee and a local school governor. He is also the Treasurer of a local cultural welfare association.

I'm keen to help create a cleaner, greener & safer environment for my family, for residents, for businesses & for the wider community.
Wallington High Street has so much potential & with some investment & a little bit of creative thinking I believe it could become a real destination.
Wallington has an amazing community & by working together we can accomplish amazing things & make a real difference.
I help organise Wallington Music Festival & Wallington Chrismas Lights & I'm already working with residents, local businesses & the police to help tackle crime in & around the high street.

John is a chartered architect who has proudly called Wallington home since 2019, having moved from central London.

He values the area’s excellent transport links, green spaces, and local services, recognising them as vital assets that should be protected and enhanced for future generations. Passionate about community and public service, John is standing as a local councillor to represent residents with energy, integrity, and a strong commitment to fairness.

He is dedicated to listening to local voices, supporting sustainable development, and helping to build a thriving, inclusive community for everyone in Wallington.

John Flowers lives in Worcester Park and is the Chair of Worcester Park & Stonecot Labour Party. He has campaigned against the imposition of LTN traffic schemes and parking schemes in Worcester Park that do not have the consent of local residents.

As  a mother, community worker of 25 years and Labour supporter, I stand as a Councillor to be a voice of the community. The community that comprises single mums, single dads, parents, families, young people, elderly, LGBT: to provide a voice and representation of individual and group needs. I myself have received support from Councillors in family and employment disputes, as well as improving local area in the past and I know what help I needed and therefore I will 100% do my best in working for my community.

I have lived in Worcester Park for 27 years.  I have three son all of whom have attended local schools.  I’m passionate about supporting young people to achieve their full potential. My youngest son currently attends Coombe Boys School, where I have been a school Governor for the last year.  Previously, I mentored primary school children from ethnic minority backgrounds at risk of exclusion.  I am a coach at New Malden Football, where I have been coaching children's football for the last 10 years. I have also been a trustee for the Sickle Cell Society for eight years.

For the previous 16 years, I worked for Chelsea & Westminster Hospital and Imperial College London, supporting new research being put into frontline NHS practice to improve services for NHS patients.

My knowledge of the NHS and research will enable me to support the improvement of GP and Hospital services in our community, and ensure that we get the full benefit of the London Cancer Hub being developed in Sutton.

I have lived in this area all my life and have deep roots in Sutton, having been born, educated, and raised my family locally. I am an IT professional with a strong belief in the value of social cohesion and the positive impact it has on building strong, resilient communities.
